name: Social-Matrix-Factorization

documentation.readme: Simple model for exploiting scoial networks data in recommender systems.
usage_sections: Example: Output of : import utils, SMF import numpy as np traindata = utils.read('trainuseritemscore.txt') validdata = utils.read('validationuseritemscore.txt') edgelist = utils.read('usersconnections.txt') MAXID = int(np.max(traindata, axis=0)[0]) LIMIT = 1 network, networktranspose = ut

arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with thewhiteh4t/seeker (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with iojw/socialscan (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with devanshbatham/ParamSpider (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='recon'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web] in the OSINT category. Fits when the engagement needs: Social-Matrix-Factorization.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Simple model for exploiting scoial networks data in recommender systems.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `iteration 0  RMSE: 1.1623740799051603`. Further invocations from upstream: `iteration 1  RMSE: 0.974779153572551`; `iteration 2  RMSE: 0.9061886948956133`.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need OSINT, use Social-Matrix-Factorization because Social-Matrix-Factorization.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use Social-Matrix-Factorization as a cataloged OSINT tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iteration 0  RMSE: 1.1623740799051603` as documented upstream for Social-Matrix-Factorization.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iteration 1  RMSE: 0.974779153572551` as documented upstream for Social-Matrix-Factorization.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iteration 2  RMSE: 0.9061886948956133` as documented upstream for Social-Matrix-Factorization.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `iteration 3  RMSE: 0.8767977965409327` as documented upstream for Social-Matrix-Factorization.
When chaining OSINT tools, pair Social-Matrix-Factorization with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `Social-Matrix-Factorization --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
